scope: This specification covers the reviewed RFC 9001 extraction outputs and preserves the distinction between RFC 9001 packet-protection behavior and overlapping TLS or transport references.
purpose: Capture the QUIC TLS and packet-protection requirements defined by RFC 9001 as canonical spec-trace clauses.
context: RFC 9001 refines TLS behavior for QUIC packet protection and key updates. The assembly keeps RFC 9001 as a single RFC-level concern while reporting overlap with RFC 9000 and TLS references in generated review output.

requirements.title: Interpret uppercase BCP 14 keywords
requirements.statement: Uppercase BCP 14 keywords in this document MUST be interpreted as described in RFC 2119 and RFC 8174 when they appear in all capitals.
notes: The sentence defines the interpretation rule for all-caps normative keywords in this document.

requirements.title: Assume packet confidentiality
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST assume responsibility for the confidentiality and integrity protection of packets.
notes: High-level packet protection responsibility stated in the introduction.

requirements.title: Derive packet keys from TLS handshake
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST use keys derived from a TLS handshake.
notes: The source sentence ties packet protection to keys derived from the TLS handshake.

requirements.title: Carry handshake and alert messages directly
requirements.statement: TLS handshake and alert messages MUST be carried directly over the QUIC transport.
notes: The source sentence contrasts this with TLS records over TCP.

requirements.title: Replace the TLS record layer
requirements.statement: QUIC transport MUST take over the responsibilities of the TLS record layer.
notes: The source sentence presents QUIC transport as replacing the TLS record layer for this traffic.

requirements.title: Rely on TLS for security-critical functions
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST rely on TLS for authentication and negotiation of parameters that are critical to security and performance.
notes: The source sentence states that TLS handles authentication and critical parameter negotiation.

requirements.title: Use the TLS handshake
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST use the TLS handshake.
notes: This is the first half of the cooperation sentence.

requirements.title: Use QUIC-provided delivery services
requirements.statement: TLS MUST use the reliability, ordered delivery, and record layer provided by QUIC.
notes: This is the second half of the cooperation sentence.

requirements.title: Send TLS messages via QUIC
requirements.statement: The TLS component MUST send and receive messages via the QUIC component.
notes: The source sentence also says QUIC provides TLS a reliable stream abstraction.

requirements.title: Provide a reliable stream abstraction
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST provide a reliable stream abstraction to TLS.
notes: This is the other half of the first interaction sentence.

requirements.title: Provide TLS updates to QUIC
requirements.statement: The TLS component MUST provide a series of updates to the QUIC component.
notes: The source sentence gives keys and state changes as examples of those updates.

requirements.title: Do not use TLS Application Data records
requirements.statement: QUIC applications that want to send data MUST NOT send it using TLS Application Data records.
notes: This is the negative form of the contrast with TLS over TCP.

requirements.title: Send data as QUIC frames
requirements.statement: QUIC applications that want to send data MUST send it as QUIC STREAM frames or other frame types carried in QUIC packets.
notes: The source sentence allows STREAM frames or other frame types; the open set is preserved.

requirements.title: Carry handshake data in CRYPTO frames
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST carry TLS handshake data in CRYPTO frames.

requirements.title: Define CRYPTO frame boundaries
requirements.statement: Each CRYPTO frame MUST consist of a contiguous block of handshake data identified by an offset and length.

requirements.title: Package and encrypt CRYPTO frames
requirements.statement: Those frames MUST be packaged into QUIC packets.

requirements.title: Package and encrypt CRYPTO frames
requirements.statement: Those frames MUST be encrypted under the current encryption level.

requirements.title: Deliver handshake data reliably
requirements.statement: Once TLS handshake data has been delivered to QUIC, QUIC MUST deliver it reliably.

requirements.title: Associate TLS-produced chunks with current keys
requirements.statement: Each chunk of data that is produced by TLS MUST be associated with the set of keys that TLS is currently using.

requirements.title: Retransmit with the same keys
requirements.statement: If QUIC needs to retransmit that data, it MUST use the same keys even if TLS has already updated to newer keys.

requirements.title: Map encryption levels to packet number spaces
requirements.statement: Each encryption level MUST correspond to a packet number space.

requirements.title: Let packet number space determine frame semantics
requirements.statement: The packet number space that is used MUST determine the semantics of frames.

requirements.title: Indicate packet keys via packet type
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST use the packet type to indicate which keys were used to protect a given packet.

requirements.title: Prefer coalesced packets
requirements.statement: When packets of different types need to be sent, endpoints SHOULD use coalesced packets to send them in the same UDP datagram.

requirements.title: Protect packets with TLS-derived keys
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST protect packets with keys derived from the TLS handshake.

requirements.title: Use the TLS-negotiated AEAD
requirements.statement: QUIC MUST use the AEAD algorithm negotiated by TLS.

requirements.title: Leave Version Negotiation packets unprotected
requirements.statement: Version Negotiation packets MUST NOT have cryptographic protection.

requirements.title: Protect Retry packets with AEAD_AES_128_GCM
requirements.statement: Retry packets MUST use AEAD_AES_128_GCM to provide protection against accidental modification and limit the entities that can produce a valid Retry.

requirements.title: Use AEAD_AES_128_GCM for Initial packets
requirements.statement: Initial packets MUST use AEAD_AES_128_GCM.

requirements.title: Derive Initial keys from the first client Initial
requirements.statement: The keys for Initial packets MUST be derived from the Destination Connection ID field of the first Initial packet sent by the client.

requirements.title: Protect all other packets cryptographically
requirements.statement: All other packets MUST have strong cryptographic protections for confidentiality and integrity using keys and algorithms negotiated by TLS.

requirements.title: Apply the same protection process to Initial packets
requirements.statement: The same packet protection process MUST be applied to Initial packets.

requirements.title: Treat Initial packets as lacking confidentiality and integrity protection
requirements.statement: Initial packets MUST NOT be considered to have confidentiality or integrity protection.

requirements.title: Use a fixed key for Retry packets
requirements.statement: Retry packets MUST use a fixed key.

requirements.title: Allow key update after handshake confirmation
requirements.statement: Once the handshake is confirmed, an endpoint MAY initiate a key update.

requirements.title: Identify packet protection keys with Key Phase
requirements.statement: The Key Phase bit MUST indicate which packet protection keys are used to protect the packet.

requirements.title: Initialize Key Phase to zero
requirements.statement: The Key Phase bit MUST initially be set to 0 for the first set of 1-RTT packets.

requirements.title: Toggle Key Phase on each update
requirements.statement: The Key Phase bit MUST be toggled to signal each subsequent key update.

requirements.title: Let Key Phase detect key changes
requirements.statement: The Key Phase bit MUST allow a recipient to detect a change in keying material without needing to receive the first packet that triggered the change.

requirements.title: Update keys when Key Phase changes
requirements.statement: An endpoint that notices a changed Key Phase bit MUST update keys.

requirements.title: Decrypt the packet with the changed Key Phase
requirements.statement: An endpoint that notices a changed Key Phase bit MUST decrypt the packet that contains the changed value.

requirements.title: Update both endpoints on key update
requirements.statement: Initiating a key update MUST result in both endpoints updating keys.

requirements.title: Prohibit TLS KeyUpdate messages
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST NOT send a TLS KeyUpdate message.

requirements.title: Treat TLS KeyUpdate as a connection error
requirements.statement: Endpoints MUST treat the receipt of a TLS KeyUpdate message as a connection error of type 0x010a, equivalent to a fatal TLS alert of unexpected_message.

requirements.title: Use caution with unauthenticated Initial data
requirements.statement: Implementations SHOULD use caution when relying on any data contained in Initial packets that is not otherwise authenticated.

requirements.title: Fail on Handshake tampering
requirements.statement: Any tampering with data carried in Handshake packets that requires modifying TLS handshake messages MUST cause the TLS handshake to fail.
notes: This restates the causal consequence in the source sentence as a normative invariant.

requirements.title: Carry QUIC transport parameters
requirements.statement: The TLS handshake MUST carry values for QUIC transport parameters.

requirements.title: Authenticate QUIC transport parameters
requirements.statement: The TLS handshake MUST authenticate values for QUIC transport parameters.

requirements.title: Apply TLS security considerations to QUIC
requirements.statement: All security considerations that apply to TLS MUST also apply to the use of TLS in QUIC.
notes: This is a scope rule for security analysis rather than a packet-processing rule.

requirements.title: Register quic_transport_parameters at codepoint 57
requirements.statement: IANA MUST register codepoint 57 (0x39) for the quic_transport_parameters extension in the TLS ExtensionType Values registry.
notes: The source sentence reports the completed registration in past tense; this clause restates the registry action normatively.

requirements.title: Mark Recommended as Yes
requirements.statement: The Recommended column for the quic_transport_parameters extension MUST be marked Yes.

requirements.title: Include CH and EE in TLS 1.3 column
requirements.statement: The TLS 1.3 column for the quic_transport_parameters extension MUST include CH (ClientHello) and EE (EncryptedExtensions).

requirements.title: Restrict larger packet-size-derived limits
requirements.statement: Only endpoints that strictly limit packet size MAY use the larger confidentiality and integrity limits derived using the smaller packet size.

requirements.title: Require AEAD usage limits
requirements.statement: Any AEAD that is used with QUIC MUST have limits on use that ensure that both confidentiality and integrity are preserved.

requirements.title: Limit GCM confidentiality at 2^11 bytes
requirements.statement: Endpoints that do not send packets larger than 2^11 bytes MUST NOT protect more than 2^28 packets in a single connection.

requirements.title: Limit GCM confidentiality at 2^16 bytes
requirements.statement: Endpoints that allow packets as large as 2^16 bytes MUST NOT protect more than 2^23 packets in a single connection.

requirements.title: Limit GCM integrity at 2^11 bytes
requirements.statement: Endpoints that do not attempt to remove protection from packets larger than 2^11 bytes MUST NOT attempt to remove protection from more than 2^57 packets.

requirements.title: Limit GCM integrity at unrestricted size
requirements.statement: Endpoints that do not restrict the size of processed packets MUST NOT attempt to remove protection from more than 2^52 packets.

requirements.title: Apply one GCM integrity limit to both functions
requirements.statement: The same integrity limit SHOULD be applied to AEAD_AES_128_GCM and AEAD_AES_256_GCM.

requirements.title: Limit CCM at 2^11 bytes
requirements.statement: Endpoints that limit packets to 2^11 bytes MUST have both confidentiality and integrity limits of 2^26.5 packets.

requirements.title: Limit CCM at unrestricted packet size
requirements.statement: Endpoints that do not restrict packet size MUST have a limit of 2^21.5 packets.

title: QUIC TLS (RFC 9001)
